sql_array: CREATE TABLE keywords (id INT PRIMARY KEY, keyword VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE xml_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data XML NOT NULL);
SELECT k.id, k.keyword, xpath_contains(data, get_keyword_ucase(k.keyword)) AS contains_keyword FROM keywords k JOIN xml_data x ON x.id = 1 WHERE get_keyword_ucase(k.keyword) LIKE '%example%';

SELECT k.keyword, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY k.keyword) AS keyword_count FROM keywords k JOIN (SELECT data FROM xml_data WHERE xpath_contains(data, get_keyword_ucase('Sample'))) AS x ON x.data IS NOT NULL GROUP BY k.keyword HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 ORDER BY keyword_count DESC;

SELECT k.keyword, CASE WHEN xpath_contains(data, get_keyword_ucase(NOT NULL)) THEN 'Exists' ELSE 'Does Not Exist' END AS keyword_status FROM keywords k JOIN xml_data x ON xpath_contains(x.data, get_keyword_ucase('Test')) WHERE k.keyword IS NOT NULL;

SELECT DISTINCT get_keyword_ucase(k.keyword) AS upper_keyword, x.data FROM keywords k JOIN xml_data x ON xpath_contains(x.data, get_keyword_ucase(k.keyword)) WHERE k.keyword IS NOT NULL AND k.keyword <> '';
CREATE TABLE temp_data (id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY, info VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW());
INSERT INTO temp_data (id, info) VALUES (1, 'First Entry'), (2, 'Second Entry');
SELECT td.info, LENGTH(td.info) AS info_length FROM temp_data td WHERE LENGTH(td.info) < 55;
ALTER TABLE keywords ADD COLUMN created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
